prompt: Why can't a QA tester verify an AI release-notes feature with a normal assertEquals test the way they would a deterministic function?
choices: The same prompt can produce different wording each run, and there is rarely one single correct output string
AI features never produce incorrect outputs, so there is nothing to test
It is impossible to measure latency or cost for AI features
Deterministic functions cannot be tested at all

prompt: Which of the following are evaluation APPROACHES (ways to gather judgments), as opposed to metrics? (Select all that apply.)
An AI summarizer sometimes adds a feature to the release note that appeared in no merged PR. Which metric most directly captures this problem?
A PM and QA tester want to ship a prompt change to an AI feature without silently degrading quality. Which practices support safe, evidence-based iteration? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Rerun a golden-dataset eval suite after the change and keep it only if scores improve with no regression
Wire the eval suite into CI as a regression gate that runs before each release
Judge the change by how good it looks on two or three hand-picked demos
Track latency and cost alongside quality so a quality gain isn't hiding a speed or cost regression
